## Overview

**v0.5.0-rc5 — release-workflow validation for the Arch Linux package and the
newly-declared package dependencies.**

No application code changes versus the v0.5.0 content. This RC exists only to
exercise two release-pipeline additions end-to-end in CI before the final tag:

1. **Arch Linux `.pkg.tar.zst`** — a new `pkg` entry in the Linux release
   matrix builds an Arch package inside an `archlinux:base-devel` container.
   Because `makepkg` refuses to run as root, `dist/linux/package.sh` builds the
   binary as root and then drops to a non-root `builder` user for `makepkg`.
   Artifact: `tildaz-0.5.0-1-x86_64.pkg.tar.zst` (Arch's official architecture
   is x86_64 only). This is the **first** time this job runs in CI.

2. **Declared package dependencies** — the `.deb` now declares
   `Depends: libxkbcommon0, libfreetype6, libfontconfig1` (+ `Recommends`), and
   the `.rpm` declares SONAME-based `Requires` for the same core libraries
   (distro-name-agnostic) plus weak `Recommends`. Previously both packages
   declared no dependencies and relied solely on runtime `dlopen`.

If every matrix entry (Windows / macOS / Linux × archs / formats, **plus the
new Arch package**) uploads successfully, the final `v0.5.0` is cut from the
same commit.

The full release content — Linux Wayland support across KDE Plasma, Hyprland,
sway, Cinnamon, GNOME and COSMIC, plus cross-platform polish — is described in
the `v0.5.0` release notes (`dist/release-notes/v0.5.0.md`).

Artifact file names use the base version (`tildaz-v0.5.0-...`) regardless of
the RC suffix; the tag page indicates which RC the files belong to.
